entity.summary: Non-custodial decentralized swap-routing and distributed-liquidity protocol whose Superposition architecture lets wallet-held assets quote and fill onchain swaps without deposits or lockups.
entity.notes: The verified-unadded candidate named BarterSwap Superposition is modeled under the current Barter protocol identity. HEI uses 2025-03-31, the documented introduction date of Superposition, as the canonical public launch marker for this exchange-like liquidity architecture. Barter's solver and native swap products remain part of the same entity.

events.title: Barter introduced Superposition
events.description: Barter introduced its distributed-liquidity architecture, allowing users to supply wallet-held liquidity to onchain swap execution without depositing assets into conventional pools.
events.notes: An independent interview preserves the exact introduction date, while the first-party white paper documents the March 2025 protocol release and architecture.

evidence.title: Barter
evidence.notes: The current first-party site exposes the live Barter application, swap router, liquidity product, documentation, and network activity.

evidence.title: Introducing Barter
evidence.notes: Current first-party documentation defines Barter as a DeFi execution protocol and Superposition as its non-custodial liquidity solution.

evidence.title: Superposition V1 White Paper
evidence.notes: The first-party paper is dated March 2025; published_at is normalized to the first day of that month because no exact publication day is printed.

evidence.title: Barter Co-Founder Nikita Ovchinnik on Liquidity Without Lockups and What DeFi Needs Next
evidence.notes: Interview states that the team introduced Superposition on March 31, 2025.

evidence.title: Wallet-Permissioned Liquidity
evidence.notes: Independent 2026 research treats Barter Superposition as a current implementation of wallet-permissioned liquidity.
